name: USS PC-1081 (PC-1081)
events: 9 Sep 1944 Around 1400 VW/9, a coordinated attack group, made up of USS Drum (T/Lt.Cdr. M.H. Rindskopf, USN), USS Sawfish (T/Cdr. A.B. Banister, USN) and USS Icefish (T/Cdr. R.W. Peterson, USN) departed from Pearl Harbor for a patrol in the South China Sea. They were first to proceed to Saipan before continuing on to their patrol area. This was the 11th war patrol for USS Drum, 8th war patrol for USS Sawfish and 1st war patrol for USS Icefish. The Commanding Officer of USS Sawfish was the leader of the group.

At the same time the submarine USS Rock (T/Cdr. J.J. Flachsenhar, USN) also departed Pearl Harbor for her 4th war patrol. Also in the South China Sea but she was not part of the coordinated attack group.
They were escorted out until around 2000 VW by the patrol vessel USS PC 1081 (Lt.(jg) J.H. Smith, USNR). At the same time USS Rock also parted company and proceeded independently.
